title: The 2026 Who's Number One 32 Results | New Champions Crowned In Austin, TX
author: Madelyn Unlock this
content: WNO 32 The 2026 Who's Number One 32 Results | New Champions Crowned In Austin, TX The 2026 WNO 32 contest took place on Mar. 31 in Austin, TX. With new champions crowned in multiple classes, here are all of the results. Apr 1, 2026 by Madelyn Mendoza Unlock this video, live events, and more with a subscription! Sign Up Already a subscriber? Log In Before the calendar switches over to April, one more grappling event was able to squeeze it's way in to the March calendar. Subscribe to Watch The Best Grappling Live FloGrappling's Official No-Gi Rankings That's right fans, Who's Number One is back and better than ever. The WNO 32 took place in Austin, Texas on the night of Mar. 31st and what a sight it was to behold. Fans who couldn't make it to the event in person could catch all of the live action on FloGrappling and can check out the replays of the event on demand on the FloGrappling website. Highlighting the night at WNO 32, new champions were crowned in the grand prix's and the super fights. With so much action, there is a lot to discuss. Here are all of the results from the Lone Star State. WNO 32 Results Prelim 1 WNO - Feather Conner Stallmer (Simple Man Martial Arts) defeats Nakoa Navarro Henderson (10th Planet Las Vegas) by forcing a submission in 6:17. Prelim 2 WNO - Straw Bebe Junco (Alpha Miami Grappling) takes down Zoe Freeman (Renzo Gracie San Antonio) by Judges decision. Feather Bracket WNO - Feather James Ortiz (Simple Man Martial Arts) defeats Chingy Sainz (Alpha Miami Grappling) by Judges Decision Semifinals James Ortiz (Simple Man Martial Arts) takes down JP Tran ( Art of Jiu-Jitsu) by Judges Decision Chingy Sainz (Alpha Miami Grappling) defeats Matthew "bam bam" Velez (Prodigy Jiu-Jitsu) by Judges Decision. Main 1 WNO - Middle Raajus Dewan (Kingsway Jiu-Jitsu) forces Austin Senos (Simple Man Martial Arts) into submission at 2:38 for the victory. Light Bracket WNO - Light Dorian Olivarez (Kingsway Jiu-Jitsu) takes home the champions over Deandre Corbe (Standard & Black Box Grappling) by Judges Decision. Semifinals Deandre Corbe (Standard & Black Box Grappling) takes down Max Hanson (Adamas & Pedigo Submission Fighting) by Judges Decision. Dorian Olivarez (Kingsway Jiu-Jitsu) forces Julian Espinosa Vienna into a submission at 1:29 for the victory. Main 4 WNO - Bantam Lilian Marchand (Atos Jiu-Jitsu) claims a victory over Janie Fragoso (Caio Terra Association) by unanimous Judges Decision. Main 5 WNO - Fly Thaynara Victoria (Aspire To Inspire) defeats Mona Bailey (Carlson Gracie North Houston & Team Bailey) by submission at 2:55. Main 6 WNO - Catch Nick Mataya (Simple Man Martial Arts) has a huge comeback victory over Jacob Bornemann (Pedigo Submission Fighting) by Submission in 9:49. Main 8 WNO - Light Heavy Felipe 'laranjinha' (Six Blades) takes down Sebastian Attard (Kingsway Jiu-Jitsu) by Judges Decision.
